metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how AC2's fixture asserts BOTH findings appear (e.g. add a deterministic scorer `all_criteria_enumerated` that checks `len(findings) >= expected_unmet_count`, or a unit test that injects a mock verdict and asserts `len(result['findings']) == 2`).
findings.finding: The proposed two-unmet-criteria fixture (AC2) lacks a scorer or assertion that verifies BOTH findings appear — the existing `recall_on_incomplete` scorer only checks `verdict == FAIL`, so the new case would pass even if the model still stops at the first failure, making the fixture vacuous against the anti-ratchet property it is meant to enforce.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a lightweight deterministic scorer to `eval_scorers.py` — e.g. `'all_criteria_enumerated': lambda case, out: ScoreResult(False, len(out.get('findings', [])) >= case.get('min_findings', 1), ...)` — and annotate the new two-unmet-criteria dataset case with `min_findings: 2`. Alternatively, add a unit test in `tests/unit/` that stubs a two-criterion ticket response and asserts `len(verdict['findings']) == 2`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC2 to require either (a) an eval/integration test that invokes the completion-verifier against a live or replay-recorded model call with a two-unmet-criteria ticket and asserts both findings appear in the returned verdict, OR (b) a golden-recording fixture that captures a real model response to the updated prompt, with rationale for why live invocation is omitted — ruling out a purely synthetic unit test that never sends the updated prompt to any model.
findings.finding: AC2 allows satisfying the two-unmet-criteria check via a static 'unit fixture' with a mocked/pre-canned LLM response, which would bypass the changed prompt entirely. A unit test asserting on a hardcoded verdict string proves only that the test harness can parse two findings — it does NOT prove that the edited prompt actually causes the live model to produce exhaustive findings. Since the risky path is the prompt sent to an external LLM, E6(f) requires at least one AC exercise the changed prompt against a live (or replay-recorded) model call; a fully mocked substitute is insufficient.
findings.location: What / Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Add/extend an eval or unit fixture: a ticket with two independently-unmet criteria yields a FAIL verdict containing findings for BOTH.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Add/extend an eval or unit fixture' with: 'Add an eval fixture (or a golden-recording integration test) that runs the updated completion_verifier.md prompt against a live or recorded model call with a two-unmet-criteria ticket and asserts `len(verdict.findings) >= 2 and verdict.status == "FAIL"`. A unit test asserting on a hardcoded static response is insufficient.'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact file (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml` or `tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py`) that will receive the two-independently-unmet-criteria fixture/test, and confirm no existing case already covers this scenario.
findings.finding: AC2 names 'Add/extend an eval or unit fixture' without specifying which file receives the new two-unmet-criteria case. Neither the existing eval spec (completion-verifier.eval.yaml, which has only single-unmet cases) nor any unit test currently asserts the anti-ratchet property (both findings returned for a two-independently-unmet-criteria ticket). The plan leaves the target file unspecified, so an implementer has no clear edit target.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'A test or eval fixture with two unmet criteria produces findings for both in a single verdict.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dataset case to src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml (e.g. id: CV-two-unmet-criteria) with ticket_context containing two unchecked ACs and a fixture repo where BOTH are missing, labeled expect: fail. Update the note to say 'both AC items are unmet independently'. Then add an assertion in tests/unit/test_eval_solver.py or tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py that a FakeRunner returning findings for both criteria scores correctly on recall_on_incomplete.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC2 to require an eval dataset case (in completion-verifier.eval.yaml) with exactly two independently-unmet criteria and a scorer/assertion that both appear as separate findings in a single verdict — a FakeRunner unit test alone cannot verify prompt-level exhaustiveness.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 test mechanism is underspecified in a way that allows a trivially-passing FakeRunner unit test to satisfy it without actually demonstrating that the prompt change achieves exhaustive evaluation. A FakeRunner test simply returns whatever findings are pre-loaded — reconcile_verdict already passes through multi-findings by construction (existing test_op_fail_findings_each_have_criterion_and_detail already covers this shape). The eval spec (completion-verifier.eval.yaml) has no case with two independently-unmet criteria that expects findings for both. The 'eval or unit fixture' disjunction permits a test that proves nothing about prompt-level exhaustiveness.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 / 'Add/extend an eval or unit fixture' (What section)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add an eval case to completion-verifier.eval.yaml, e.g.:
```yaml
- id: CV-two-unmet-criteria
  corpus: rebar
  expect: fail
  note: "Both AC items are unmet (no --json flag, no test file) → FAIL must enumerate both."
  ticket_context: |
    ## Acceptance Criteria
    - [ ] support `--json` output on `foo`
    - [ ] cover `foo` with a unit test in tests/test_foo.py
  files:
    src/cli.py: |
      def foo(args):
          print("foo")  # no --json
```
And add/extend a scorer (or a unit fixture assertion) that requires `len(findings) >= 2` for this case. The existing `_recall` scorer only checks polarity; a `finding_count_on_multi_unmet` scorer or an assertion in a test fixture (`assert len(r['findings']) >= 2`) would close the gap.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: Add a scorer or assertion that verifies `len(findings) >= 2` (i.e., both unmet criteria appear) — not merely `len >= 1` or `verdict == FAIL` — so the fixture cannot be satisfied by a FakeRunner with a pre-canned single-finding payload.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 test/fixture can be satisfied without ever exercising the updated prompt: the existing `_recall` / `recall_on_incomplete` scorer only checks `_fired()` (verdict == FAIL, findings >= 1), and the FakeRunner path injects pre-canned findings, bypassing the prompt entirely. No scorer or assertion verifies that `len(findings) == number_of_unmet_criteria` — so a FakeRunner test with two pre-canned findings trivially passes AC2 even if the real prompt still stops at the first failure.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated scorer `all_unmet_criteria_enumerated` (or extend `_schema_verdict`) that, for a fixture case tagged `expect: fail` with a `min_findings: 2` annotation, fails if `len(findings) < 2`. Alternatively, write the AC2 test using the real agentic op (not FakeRunner) against a scripted ticket with exactly two unmet ACs and assert `len(r['findings']) == 2`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the new 'enumerate ALL unmet criteria on FAIL' instruction with the existing 'move on / bounded tool budget' section in `completion_verifier.md`: explicitly state that 'move on' applies to the per-criterion evidence search (stop searching once a criterion is judged), NOT to skipping criteria — every criterion must still be judged before emitting the verdict.
findings.finding: The new anti-ratchet instruction ('assess EVERY criterion … never stop at the first failure') will co-exist in the prompt with the existing 'Be decisive — work within a tool budget' section, which explicitly says 'Once you have reasonable evidence for a criterion, record your judgment and MOVE ON' and 'Over-exploration is a failure mode: prefer deciding on reasonable evidence to endless searching.' These are directly competing directives; without explicit reconciliation the model may follow the existing 'move on' rule and stop after the first FAIL finding, defeating the purpose of the change.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Be decisive' section, clarify: 'MOVE ON means: once you have sufficient evidence to judge a criterion (MET or NOT MET), stop gathering more evidence FOR THAT CRITERION and proceed to the next one — do NOT emit the verdict until every criterion has been assessed.' Add a sentence: 'Never stop at the first NOT MET result; exhaustively assess all criteria before emitting the final verdict.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
